feedback: The proposal accurately captures the load-bearing tolerance logic from the bash phase modules and provides good coverage of every bats scenario. Specifically: C4-11 covers all four decision verbs (accepted/accept/approved/approve) across all three field names (.decision/.reviewVerdict/.verdict) case-insensitively, matching the jq pipeline in contract.sh:66. C4-12 covers all four criteria-count shapes from contract.sh:43-48 (.criteria, .features[].acceptanceCriteria, .features[].criteria, flat .acceptanceCriteria) plus the 0 default. C4-27 covers all three result fields from evaluator.sh:46 with pass/passed case-insensitive. C4-28 covers the four-variant chains for passCount/failCount/blockingFailures from evaluator.sh:48-50. C4-18 explicitly pins the load-bearing return code 2 for blocked status (generator.sh:47). C4-13 covers the max-rounds fallback (contract.sh:81-82) including the equality-with-latest-proposal check. C4-32 enforces the no-.sh/.ps1-modification constraint, and the outOfScope list reinforces this. Every numbered @test in the four bats files maps to a Python criterion: planner bats (5 tests) -> C4-01/02/03/04 plus C4-05 design-spec; contract bats (5 tests) -> C4-06/07/08/09/10; generator bats (5 tests) -> C4-15/16/17/18/19; evaluator bats (5 tests) -> C4-23/24/25/26/30. C4-34 makes parity an explicit check. Regression sprints [1,2,3] match the dependsOn list in sprint-plan.json. The criteria are testable with concrete steps and scenario names. Approved as-is.
advisoryNotes: C4-28 specifies 'six stub eval-reports each using a different field-name variant', but pass/fail/blocking each have four variants (12 total). The generator may want to either expand to 12 stubs or document why six is sufficient (e.g., one per variant per metric covering the camelCase/snake_case/score.* axes). Not blocking; the field-name list itself is correct.

C4-13 asserts contract.json equals the last proposal and return == 0 but does not pin the call count to exactly MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS. File equality plus the return code is sufficient evidence of the fallback path; consider adding a generator-call-count assertion for robustness during implementation.

C4-21 correctly mirrors generator.sh:25 which injects design-spec.md unconditionally when the file exists (not gated on project type). Keep this behaviour identical -- it is the bash version's actual behaviour, not the apparent intent.
